name: OrbitKit
tagline: Product operations for modern AI teams
description: OrbitKit helps teams collect feedback, prioritize roadmap ideas, and publish changelogs from one workspace.
highlights: Single JSON entry powers archive card and detail page content.
Designed for static export so it can ship through GitHub Pages.
Leaves room for editorial copy instead of forcing rigid schemas everywhere.

sections.title: Why it fits the template
content: OrbitKit demonstrates the kind of product record this starter is optimized for: structured enough to generate rich landing pages, but flexible enough to support real editorial storytelling.
The detail layout uses a strong hero, content panels, and a compact sidebar so a product page can rank for its own terms without needing a CMS or database.

sections.title: Suggested page modules
content: Use sections like features, integrations, pricing notes, and use cases as optional content blocks inside the JSON model or a companion markdown file.
That keeps your build pipeline simple while preserving enough freedom to write pages that do not look programmatically assembled.

name: Stacklane
description: Stacklane turns fragmented docs and integration references into a searchable portal for engineering teams.
highlights: Strong fit for category archive pages targeting tooling terms.
Shows how pricing and status fields can stay compact in cards.
Provides enough metadata for related-product blocks and sidebars.

sections.title: Content strategy
content: The archive page can target broad developer-tool keywords while the product detail page handles long-tail branded and capability terms.
This split mirrors the strongest part of the upstream theme: clear page intent and strong internal linking between collections and detail pages.

name: SignalPad
description: SignalPad combines event summaries, release notes, and experiment insights in a static-friendly reporting layer.
highlights: Useful example for analytics or observability category pages.
Structured enough for schema validation in CI.
Simple fields can expand later into comparison tables or editorial blocks.

sections.title: Archive positioning
content: SignalPad shows how to pitch a product briefly on the card while reserving depth for the detail page. That helps avoid overcrowding category archives.
If you later add blog content, products like this can link into supporting editorial posts or comparison articles.

name: FlowPatch
description: FlowPatch packages reusable automations, integration recipes, and operational runbooks in a portable directory format.
highlights: Good example of a page that can rank for workflow and template terms.
Pairs well with category archives that need strong cross-linking.
Can be extended with use-case sections without changing the shell.

sections.title: Extensibility
content: A product like FlowPatch benefits from optional field groups for templates, integrations, or setup effort. The current shell leaves room for those additions.
Because the layout is static-first, you can keep the deployment target on GitHub Pages instead of introducing platform-specific hosting requirements.

name: ModelDock
tagline: Evaluate prompts and datasets in one place
description: ModelDock gives AI product teams a clean place to compare prompts, evaluate responses, and document experiments.
highlights: Shows a second product in the same category for related links.
Supports a richer content section with experiments and benchmarks.
Fits the same archive and detail templates without new UI work.

sections.title: Detail page depth
content: ModelDock is the kind of listing that benefits from optional rich sections like methodology, integrations, or case studies.
The page template already separates overview, body content, and structured metadata so those additions stay organized.

name: Docframe
tagline: Publish structured docs sites from plain files
description: Docframe turns markdown, JSON, and screenshots into a clean documentation site suitable for APIs, changelogs, and product directories.
highlights: Another example of a product detail page for the developer category.
Good candidate for documentation-heavy editorial sections.
Works with static export and repository-based publishing.

sections.title: Publishing model
content: Docframe demonstrates how the same starter can support both directories and documentation-led product sites.
If you later add a blog or comparison content, the visual language still holds together because the card and section system is consistent.
